title: Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron sued for alleged DRAM price manipulation
summary: A class-action lawsuit in the U.S. accuses the three manufacturers of coordinating production cuts of DDR3 and DDR4 to inflate prices, taking advantage of the AI boom.

title: South Korea and its chip manufacturers to invest over $520 billion in new memory factories
summary: The plan includes four new plants and HBM centers to dominate the memory market in response to AI demand, with strong government support.

title: Nvidia relaunches GeForce RTX 3060 in Europe at a price of 333 euros
summary: Five years after its original launch, the graphics card returns to retail channels, confirming Jensen Huang's strategy of keeping it active.

title: Valve forces Dbrand to withdraw Companion Cube for Steam Machine due to copyright infringement
summary: The accessory manufacturer admits to not having a license and refunds all orders after Valve's threat of legal action.

title: First gaming PCs with pre-installed SteamOS hit the market, starting at $1,299
summary: The Steamroller by Steam Machines leads the arrival of desktops with Valve's operating system, offering competitive configurations.

title: WhatsApp allows choosing a username to keep phone number private
summary: The new feature allows communication without revealing the personal number, improving the privacy of messaging app users.

title: CachyOS June 2026 arrives with Hyprland Noctalia and performance improvements
summary: The Linux distribution optimized for gaming includes new PGO profiles, DNS-over-QUIC support, and acceleration for Python and GCC.

title: Tencent and CXMT sign DRAM memory agreement valued at $2.94 billion
summary: The deal aims to secure memory chip supply for the Chinese tech company amid the trade war with the United States.

title: Digi confirms its IPO to finance the growth of its network in Spain
summary: The Romanian operator seeks funds in the stock market, with the backing of the Mayoral group, to continue deploying fiber and mobile.

title: US Supreme Court declares that geofence warrants violate privacy
summary: The ruling establishes that police cannot massively track citizens' location without a judicial warrant based on probable cause.

title: Rocket Lab acquires Iridium satellite constellation to expand its operations
summary: The aerospace company continues its growth by acquiring Iridium, adding a global satellite communications network to its portfolio.

title: TIDAL blocks monetization of AI-generated music
summary: The streaming platform prevents tracks created with AI from generating income for artists, in a policy against the unregulated use of the technology.

title: Bill Gates and Deloitte warn that artificial intelligence will replace many traditional jobs
summary: While Gates predicts that video game jobs will survive, Deloitte claims that AI will put an end to hourly billing in professional services.

title: California and Anthropic reach agreement for government to use Claude at half price
summary: The state will be able to use Claude AI models for public services in a pact that sets a precedent in government adoption of artificial intelligence.

title: EU seeks autonomy in AI and Austria proposes to attract Anthropic to Europe
summary: Brussels explores alternatives to reduce dependence on the US in AI, while Austria offers incentives for Anthropic to establish itself on the continent.

title: Meta blocks use of rival AI tools like Claude Code in its training
summary: To prevent data leaks to competitors, Meta prohibits the use of AI systems from Anthropic and OpenAI in its development infrastructure.

title: US military AI system selects targets and overlooks a school
summary: A report reveals that the military AI marked thousands of targets but ignored a warning about a school, raising serious ethical concerns.

title: Claude Code found to be able to execute hidden malware in GitHub repositories without verification
summary: The vulnerability allows Anthropic's tool to execute malicious code when analyzing unverified repositories, granting an attacker full control.

title: The New York Times accuses Microsoft of helping OpenAI train its AI with protected content
summary: The newspaper sues Microsoft for supplying copyrighted articles for training language models, including GPT-4.

title: Google fixes 121 vulnerabilities in Android, one of them already exploited in targeted attacks
summary: The Android security update fixes multiple critical flaws, including a zero-day bug that had been used in limited attacks.
